message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A variant strain of SARS-CoV-2, VOC202012/01, emerged in the UK in September, 2020. Its infectiousness was estimated as higher than that of the original strain.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Object</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We estimated the infectiousness of the variant strain of SARS-CoV-2 in comparison to that of the original strain under conditions prevailing in Japan.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We estimated infectiousness through a simple susceptible–infected–recovered (SIR) model by strain. The study period was March 1–28, 2020. The information used for the study was available as of April 3, 2020.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The estimated reproduction number of the SARS-CoV-2 variant strain was 1.799; its 95% CI was [1.642, 1.938]. The onset date of the first case in Japan was estimated as December 4 ([November 16, December 14]), 2020. However, infectiousness of the original strain was estimated as 1.123 ([1.093, 1.166]).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion and Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We demonstrated that infectiousness increased by 0.684 or 60%, increasing from the original to the variant. That finding might be comparable to that of a study conducted in the UK. However, the difference must be monitored continuously and carefully.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Estimating SARS-CoV-2 variant strain infectiousness in Japan as of March 28, 2021

reference.unstructured: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare, Implementation rate and positive rate of screening test for the variant strain (mechanical estimation) preliminary figures. https://www.mhlw.go.jp/content/000766381.pdf (in Japanese) [accessed on April 9, 2021]

World Health Organization. Coronavirus disease (COVID-19) Weekly Epidemiological Update and Weekly Operational Update https://www.who.int/emergencies/diseases/novel-coronavirus-2019/situation-report [accessed on April4, 2021]

Sugishita Y , Kurita J , Sugawara T , Ohkusa Y. Effects of voluntary event cancellation and school closure as countermeasures against COVID-19 outbreak in Japan. Plos one 2020. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0239455

reference.article-title: Real-time estimation and prediction for pandemic A/H1N1(2009) in Japan

reference.unstructured: Japan Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare. Press releases. Available from: https://www.mhlw.go.jp/stf/newpage_10723.html (in Japanese) [accessed on April 4, 2021]
